title: Restrictions on Use of Unicode and Installable Character Sets
breadcrumbs: Restrictions on Use of Unicode and Installable Character Sets
text: Restrictions on Use of Unicode and Installable Character Sets Although Uniface supports Unicode and the installable character sets, there are restrictions in using them. ASCII characters are allowed anywhere. The following tables show where the full Unicode character set (as opposed to the BMP), and the installable character set can be used. Table 1. Unicode Support in the Development Objects Object Full Range Unicode Installable Character Set Model, entity and field names. No Yes All other object names, such as form names. No No Entity abbreviation No No Component subtype No No Description property of all objects No Yes Comments property of all objects Yes Yes ProcScript Yes Yes Labels Yes Yes Menu items Yes Yes ValRep pairs in widgets Yes Yes Initial Value of String fields Yes Yes Value of String fields with W and U* packing code Yes Yes Value of String fields with C and U n packing code No Yes Value of String fields in Language Set Up Yes Yes Contents of translation tables Yes Yes Edit Text File Yes Yes Table 2. Unicode Support in the Runtime Environment Uniface Items Full Range Unicode Installable Character Set CHUI (Character-based user interface) No Yes Profile characters No Yes Uniface Router Monitor No No Uniface Security Driver No No Table 3. Unicode Support in Files File Full Range Unicode Installable Character Set File names Yes Yes Contents of assignment files Yes Yes Contents of files for file operation such as fileload and filedump Yes Yes Transcript Window in Microsoft Windows Yes Not applicable Table 4. Unicode Support in Printing Printing Module Full Range Unicode Installable Character Set Enhanced printing in Windows Yes Yes Enhanced printing on non-Windows platform No Yes Regular printing No Yes DBMS Functionality Full Range Unicode Installable Character Set For detailed information on the Unicode support offered by databases, consult the respective database documentation and the Uniface DBMS Connector documentation. Table 5. Unicode support in DBMS Functionality DBMS Functionality Full Range Unicode Installable Character Set sql ProcScript instruction Yes for DB2, Oracle, MySQL, SQLite, Sybase, and TXTNote No for other connectors Yes SQL Workbench Yes for DB2, Oracle, MySQL, SQLite, Sybase, and TXTNote No for other connectors Yes Error messages Yes for DB2Note , Oracle, SQLite, and MySQL No for other connectors Yes Database table names Yes for DB2 and MySQLNote No for other connectors Yes Database names, user names, and passwords, including system user names and passwords Yes for Oracle and MySQL No for other connectors Yes Parameters of stored procedures No, except in DB2 and Sybase No, except in DB2 and Sybase, DBMS service stored procedures Yes for Sybase and OracleNote No for other connectors No Table 6. Unicode Support in Integration Functionality Integration Module Supported Location Full Range Unicode Installable Character Set C Call-In and Call-Out: Passing String parameters Yes Yes COM Call-In and Call-Out in Windows: Passing String parameters Yes Not applicable Java Call-In: Passing String parameters Yes Not applicable WebService Call-In and Call-Out: Passing String parameters Yes Not applicable XML Import/export Yes Not applicable UPOPMAIL: Message text and subject of sent and received email messages Yes Yes UHTTP components Yes Yes Was this topic helpful?
